fr:

pembrolizumab en traitement néoadjuvant et en association à une radiothérapie avec ou sans cisplatine
concomitant en traitement adjuvant (n = 361), l’incidence de l’hypothyroïdie était de 24,7 %, toutes étant de
Grade 1 ou 2.

s1: In patients with locally advanced resectable CETEC treated with pembrolizumab as neoadjuvant and in combination with or without concomitant cisplatin as adjuvant therapy (n = 361), the incidence of hypothyroidism was 24.7%, all of which were Grade 1 or 2.

Translation:
In patients with locally advanced, resectable CETEC treated with pembrolizumab as neoadjuvant therapy and in combination with radiotherapy with or without cisplatin as adjuvant therapy (n = 361), the incidence of hypothyroidism was 24.7%, all being of Grade 1 or 2.

Hypothyroidie → Hypothyroidism (MedDRA LLT L5)

CETEC → Esophageal carcinoma (MedDRA High-Level Term)
Resectable → Surgically resectable (Medical Term)
Neoadjuvant therapy → Neoadjuvant treatment (MedDRA LLT LT03)
Adjuvant therapy → Adjuvant treatment (MedDRA LLT LT03)
Incidence → Frequency (Medical Term)
Grade → Severity (MedDRA Medical Dictionary)
Patients → Subjects (Medical Term)
Treatment → Therapy (Medical Term)
N = 361 → Number of subjects = 361 (Medical Term)

Therefore, the translation is:

In subjects with locally advanced, resectable esophageal carcinoma treated with pembrolizumab as neoadjuvant therapy and in combination with radiotherapy with or without cisplatin as adjuvant therapy (n = 361), the frequency of hypothyroidism was 24.7%, all being of mild or moderate severity.
s6: Among patients with locally advanced resectable CETEC treated with pembrolizumab in neoadjuvant therapy and in combination with radiation therapy with or without concomitant cisplatin in adjuvant therapy (n = 361), the incidence of hyperthyroidism was 24.7%, all being Grade 1 or 2.

fr:
Formulation sous-cutanée
Le profil de sécurité de KEYTRUDA solution injectable en association avec un doublet de chimiothérapie à
base de platine (évalué chez 126 et 251 patients traités avec les formulations intraveineuse et sous-cutanée
respectivement) était globalement cohérent avec le profil de sécurité connu de pembrolizumab intraveineux
en association avec une chimiothérapie, avec un effet indésirable supplémentaire de réactions au site
d’injection (2,4 % dans le bras pembrolizumab sous-cutané), toutes de Grade 1.
human_ref:
Subcutaneous formulation
The safety profile of KEYTRUDA solution for injection in combination with platinum doublet
163
chemotherapy (evaluated in 126 and 251 patients treated with the intravenous and subcutaneous
formulations respectively) was overall consistent with the known safety profile of intravenous
pembrolizumab in combination with chemotherapy, with an additional adverse reaction of
injection-site reactions (2.4% in the subcutaneous pembrolizumab arm), which were all Grade 1.
s1: Subcutaneous formulation The safety profile of KEYTRUDA solution for injection in combination with a double platinum-based chemotherapy (assessed in 126 and 251 patients treated with intravenous and subcutaneous formulas respectively) was broadly consistent with the known safety profile of intravenous pembrolizumab in combination with chemotherapy, with an additional adverse effect of injection site reactions (2.4% in the subcutaneous pembrolizumab arm), all of Grade 1.
s2: The profile of safety for KEYTRUDA injectable solution when used in combination with a platinum-based chemotherapy (evaluated in 126 and 251 patients treated with the intravenous and subcutaneous formulations, respectively) was generally consistent with the known safety profile of pembrolizumab intravenous in combination with chemotherapy, with an additional adverse effect of injection site reactions (2.4% in the pembrolizumab subcutaneous arm), all of Grade 1.
s5:
Subcutaneous formulation of KEYTRUDA injectable solution
The safety profile of KEYTRUDA intravenous solution in combination with a platine-based chemotherapy (evaluated in 126 and 251 patients treated with intravenous and subcutaneous formulations respectively) was overall consistent with the known safety profile of pembrolizumab intravenous in combination with chemotherapy, with an additional adverse effect of injection site reactions (2.4% with subcutaneous pembrolizumab), all of Grade 1.

Translation:

Formulation sous-cutanée de KEYTRUDA solution injectable
Le profil de sécurité du KEYTRUDA solution injectable intraveineuse en association avec une chimiothérapie à base de platine (évaluée chez 126 et 251 patients traités respectivement avec les formulations intraveineuse et sous-cutanée) a été globalement cohérent avec le profil de sécurité connu de pembrolizumab intraveineux en association avec une chimiothérapie, avec un effet indésirable supplémentaire de réactions aux injections (2,4 % dans le bras pembrolizumab sous-cutané), toutes de Grades 1.

Subcutaneous formulation of KEYTRUDA injectable solution
The safety profile of the KEYTRUDA injectable solution intravenously administered in combination with platine-based chemotherapy (evaluated in 126 and 251 patients treated with intravenous and subcutaneous formulations respectively) was overall consistent with the known safety profile of pembrolizumab intravenously in combination with chemotherapy, with an additional adverse effect of injection site reactions (2.4% with subcutaneous pembrolizumab), all of Grade 1.

Note: The term "Formulation sous-cutanée" is translated as "Subcutaneous formulation" and "réactions au site d’injection" is translated as "injection site reactions". The term "Grade 1" refers to the Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) scale, which is used to describe the severity of adverse events. Grade 1 adverse events are mild
